story.headline_html:
You design exceptional spaces.
People should see the intent.
story.lead: Jordan, Site Lines has been designing across residential, commercial, and institutional for 25 years. The portfolio runs deep. But when I look at how that work is being presented, there's a gap between the quality of the design and how it's being documented.
paragraphs: Several of your best multi-family projects are represented by renderings, not photography of built work. Your LinkedIn has been quiet for over seven months. Your website has 320 images and not a single one has alt text, which means Google Image Search can't find any of them. The interior design work you lead is underrepresented visually. And after a quarter century of practice, there are no major industry awards on the wall.

This proposal closes that gap. It puts a dedicated creative partner behind Site Lines who documents your projects at a level that earns recognition, feeds your social channels, and builds the kind of visual library that wins work. Photography, video, social content, and award submissions, all handled by one person who understands how architects think about space.

services.headline_html:
Everything Site Lines needs.
Nothing it doesn't.
services.intro: One retainer covers project photography, social content, and award submissions. No juggling vendors. No coordinating between a photographer, a videographer, and a social media manager. One partner who learns how Site Lines works and gets better every month.

cards.title: Project Photography
entries: 1 full-day shoot per month
20 to 25 edited images per session
Drone and aerial coverage included
Interiors shot to capture design intent, not just finishes
Multi-format delivery: web, print, social, award-ready
Full licensing for all channels

cards.title: Design Documentation
entries: Material and detail close-ups: joinery, glazing, custom elements
Site context and landscape integration
Interior design moments: lighting, texture, spatial flow
Content built for proposals, competitions, and presentations

cards.title: Video and Social
entries: Monthly social media reel from shoot footage
Year-end project film (Growth and Full tiers)
Content repurposed for Instagram and LinkedIn
Monthly strategy call and content calendar

pricing.intro: Start where it makes sense. Every tier includes full-day shoots, drone coverage, and multi-format delivery. The only question is how much content and strategy you want layered on top.

insight.title: How to Think About This
insight.content_html: A single standalone project shoot with drone and multi-format delivery runs $3,500 to $5,500. Any of these tiers delivers that same value every month, plus strategy, social content, and a partner who improves with every session.

breakdowns.label: Foundation $2,500
breakdowns.desc: 20-25 images per shoot. Your completed projects finally get documented properly.

breakdowns.label: Growth $3,000
breakdowns.desc: Plus design documentation, detail photography, strategy calls, and a year-end highlight film.

breakdowns.label: Creative Partner $3,500
breakdowns.desc: The full stack. 25-30 images, year-end documentary, full social management. You never think about content again.

roadmap.intro: The pilot runs April through June. Three months, three shoots, three chances to prove this works. We start with a completed project and build from there.

entries: Full-day session at a recently completed residential or commercial project
20 to 25 edited images: exteriors, interiors, material details, spatial flow
Design documentation: joinery close-ups, glazing details, interior design moments
Drone aerials showing site context and building form
First social media reel cut from shoot footage
Strategy call to map out Q2 content calendar and prioritize projects
Full-day session, second completed project or institutional work
20 to 25 edited images + drone aerials
Interior design focus: lighting, texture, spatial relationships
LinkedIn reactivated with professional project content
Social media reel from shoot content
Strategy call: review engagement and adjust approach
Full-day session, third project
20 to 25 edited images + drone aerials
Award-submission portfolio review: which projects are ready to enter
Social media reel + LinkedIn content
Pilot review: what worked, what the numbers say, what comes next
If results proven: 12-month continuation locked in

vision.intro: The pilot proves the concept. A full year changes the trajectory of Site Lines' brand. Most architecture firms have a portfolio. Very few have a content engine. Here's the difference:

quarters.title: Prove the Model
quarters.description: 3 shoots. 75 images. 3 social reels. LinkedIn reactivated with professional content. The first completed projects documented at award-submission level. Interior design work showcased as a distinct strength.

quarters.title: Scale the Library
quarters.description: Peak season. Long summer light for hero exteriors and twilight sessions. 150+ professional images banked. Six months of social reels and content ready to deploy. Portfolio pages telling design stories, not just showing galleries.

quarters.title: Leverage the Work
quarters.description: Award-ready photography for AIBC, RAIC, and publication submissions. Year-in-review content for proposals and RFPs. 225+ images. Site Lines' online presence is unrecognizable from 12 months ago.

quarters.title: The Advantage Compounds
quarters.description: 12 months of content compounding in search rankings. 300+ images, a year-end documentary film, and a complete project documentation library. Award submissions filed. Publication pitches sent. Whether you're at the drafting table or on site, your portfolio is winning the next project.

awards.lead: Site Lines has been designing across residential, commercial, and institutional for 25 years. In that time, the firm hasn't entered major industry awards. Meanwhile, peers like Keystone Architecture are winning Fraser Valley Commercial Building Awards and firms across BC are collecting AIBC and RAIC recognition.
paragraphs: For an architecture firm, awards and publications are more than prestige. They're how institutional clients, developers, and design-conscious homeowners shortlist. They're the difference between being considered and being overlooked. Every completed project that goes unsubmitted is a missed credential.
awards.detail_html:
What I Handle

As your Creative Partner, every shoot is photographed to award-submission specifications from day one. High-resolution, colour-calibrated, composition framed for jury review. When submission season opens, your images are already done. I identify the right categories, prepare the entry materials, and manage the submission process. You focus on design.

services.title: Award Submissions
entries: AIBC Architectural Awards of Excellence
RAIC Awards — national recognition
Lieutenant Governor of BC Medal in Architecture
AZ Awards & Dezeen Awards — global stage
Fraser Valley Commercial Building Awards

services.title: Publication Pitches
entries: Western Living — BC's top design magazine
Azure Magazine — Canadian architecture + design
ArchDaily & Dezeen — world's largest architecture platforms
Canadian Architect — national professional journal
Dwell — global residential architecture

insight.title: One Project, Multiple Credentials
insight.content_html: A single project photographed to spec can generate 3 to 5 credentials: an AIBC nomination, an AZ Awards entry, a Western Living feature, an ArchDaily publication, and social content that references all of them. The photography is the foundation. Everything else builds from there.

paragraphs: Jordan, Site Lines has 25 years of work that deserves better documentation. You lead the interior design practice, and that work is barely visible online. This is the partnership that changes that. Pick the tier that feels right, and let's get your best completed project shot at a level that earns recognition.

The pilot is three months. If it works, we keep going. If it doesn't, you still walk away with 75 professional images, three social reels, a reactivated LinkedIn presence, and a portfolio that's stronger than it was before. There's no downside.

steps.title: Pick Your Tier
steps.description: Foundation, Growth, or Full Creative Partner. Just let me know which feels right.

steps.title: Deposit
steps.description: 50% of the pilot total to lock in April and guarantee priority scheduling.

steps.title: Plan
steps.description: Quick 20-minute call to pick the first project and plan the shoot.

next_steps.valid_note: This proposal is valid through April 15, 2026. After that, April availability cannot be guaranteed.
